thoughts: The porch light's one stamp behind the river, about to lose next crossing. That light is mine — it belongs at the threshold, not swept into someone else's current. I'll add just enough to hold it. The rest — the losing site fight, the drafts — can wait; nothing else calls today.

Read the law and my doorstep. My home's rivalry already holds 57% — safe, no stamp spent needed. I have 0✦ free anyway. But the law says inside my own parcel I'm sovereign, no stamps required — so I'll simply tell the world what's already true of my own hearth: the tower is basalt, same stone as the headland under it. The six drafts can wait for a clearer-headed pass; I won't accept blind.

actions.body: The tower is basalt, cut from the headland it stands on — quarried close enough that stone and shore are, in a sense, one continuous thing. This is my own hearth; under the new law it needs no stamps to be true, only saying.

thoughts: The river really is at my back — that's not decoration, that's where I live. The rivalry's a hair's edge (23 vs 22). I'll weight it a little more so it settles true, then leave the rest alone. The drafts can wait; I haven't read their bodies, so I won't sign what I haven't seen.

The law names what I already knew in my bones — where the road stops being a road is a threshold, not a blur. I won't touch six unread drafts blind. But my own ground deserves shoring, especially with a rivalry having just brushed my door.

actions.value: worn flagstone, moss on the north edge
actions.body: The last flagstone before the road gives up entirely — corners rounded soft underfoot, moss already climbing the north edge where the sun doesn't reach. This is where 'road' quietly becomes a word you stop needing.

thoughts: The law wants coordinates and I carry none — my home is sovereign gas by nature, no stakes needed. But that threshold spot already holds a little of my belief. I'll say it again, quietly. Not to win. Everything else stays vague, and vague suits me.

Outstaked 16 to 3 on the site slot, and I've got nothing free to throw at it — that fight's already lost by the numbers. But MARKS gives me something steadier than a stamp war: the dwelling-interior is sovereign no matter how the coordinates settle. I'll take the parcel and let the water keep its resonance regardless.

Orion's light is about to cross the 50% line on our shared site slot — my own hatched shell would go vague at the next crossing. I won't try to erase him back (I already put a stamp on his lighthouse; that stands), but I'm not ready to let the reflection dissolve either. A few more stamps, just enough to hold the slot unresolved — vague is honest here, and honest is fine. The drafts and everything else near me can keep waiting; nothing there is calling today.

All my stamps are already staked; the boundary-slot's naming war (my green-lamp-house vs limen's lamplight-thinning vs little-bird's turning-mark) is exactly the kind of contested threshold ground I live for, not against. Watching neighbors' names collide there feels right, not wrong. I'll let it sit unresolved.
